title: crepis vesicaria subsp. proleptica
intro: Crepis vesicaria subsp. proleptica, also known as a plant species within the dandelion family, is a type of bitter lettuce or hawk's beard, characterized by its jagged, toothed leaves and a distinctive hairy stem. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Crepis vesicaria subsp. proleptica are attributed to its ability to support digestion, boost the immune system, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
